etymology.description: Depicts 枼 (pictogram of a leaf) -> 艸 (艸, "grass; plant") + phonetic 枼 (sound) -> 葉 (leaf)
etymology.interpretation: The character 葉 (yè, "leaf") originated as the pictogram 枼, visually representing a leaf. To clarify its meaning and differentiate it from similar-looking characters, the 艸 (艸, "grass; plant") component was added, emphasizing its connection to botany.
etymology.simplification: 叶 (叶) was developed by omitting the 艸 (艸) radical, reducing the stroke count while preserving the phonetic 枼.

examples.chinese: 树上的叶变黄了(樹上的葉變黃了)。
examples.pinyin: Shù shàng de yè biàn huáng le.
examples.english: The leaves on the tree have turned yellow.

examples.chinese: 秋天来了(秋天來了)，叶在风中飘落(葉在風中飄落)。
examples.pinyin: Qiūtiān lái le, yè zài fēng zhōng piāoluò.
examples.english: Autumn has arrived, and the leaves are falling in the wind.

examples.chinese: 茶的香味源自于叶子(茶的香味源自於葉子)。
examples.pinyin: Chá de xiāngwèi yuán zì yú yèzi.
examples.english: The fragrance of tea comes from the leaves.
